executiveSummary: The OryMart application currently exists as a high-fidelity prototype rather than the requested enterprise-grade, massively scalable platform. While the frontend demonstrates a modern, responsive, and immersive user interface utilizing React, Zustand, and Tailwind CSS, the backend is fundamentally unsuited for production. It relies on an in-memory data structure, simulating network latency via timeouts, and lacks a persistent database. Critical security vulnerabilities, such as a hardcoded JWT secret and absent input validation, pose severe risks. Furthermore, several extensive requirements from US-001 (e.g., personalized recommendations, language/currency toggles, and functional search) are either missing or implemented as UI-only mocks. A significant architectural overhaul of the backend is required before considering production deployment.

testCases.description: Verify user registration and login flow with valid and invalid credentials.
testCases.notes: API accepts registration but lacks input validation (e.g., password complexity, email format). In-memory DB resets on server restart, causing intermittent test failures in CI/CD.

testCases.description: Verify that adding a product to the cart updates the Zustand store and reflects the correct total.
testCases.notes: Cart state updates correctly. However, stock validation is missing; users can add more items than the available 'stock' property in the mock DB.

testCases.description: Verify the checkout process, including payment intent creation and order confirmation.
testCases.notes: Checkout flow works in the UI, but the backend generates a mock Stripe client secret using Math.random(). No actual payment gateway integration exists.

testCases.description: Verify homepage accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA compliance) including keyboard navigation and screen reader support.
testCases.notes: Custom Input and Button components lack comprehensive ARIA attributes. Some gallery images have empty alt tags. Color contrast on placeholder text (#C5C6C7 on #1F2833) needs verification.

testCases.description: Verify that the search bar filters products based on user input.
testCases.notes: Search input is present in the Header component but is not wired to any state or backend API. It is purely cosmetic.

codeAudit: BACKEND ARCHITECTURE & SECURITY: The Node.js/Express backend is a prototype. It uses an in-memory object (`const db = {...}`) which will result in total data loss upon server restart and cannot scale horizontally. Security is severely compromised by a hardcoded JWT secret (`JWT_SECRET = 'orymart_super_secret_key_2024'`) in `server.js`. There is zero input validation or sanitization on incoming requests (e.g., `req.body` in `/api/v1/auth/register`), leaving the application vulnerable to NoSQL/SQL injection (if a DB were attached) and XSS. Rate limiting and CORS configurations are overly permissive. FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE: The React frontend is well-structured. Zustand is used effectively for global state management (Cart, Auth, App). The use of Framer Motion provides the requested 'immersive' experience. However, the application lacks Error Boundaries to catch React rendering errors. API calls are made using a custom `fetcher` which is good, but lacks retry logic or robust timeout handling. The UI implements many of the US-001 requirements visually, but functional requirements like localization, real-time inventory checks, and personalized recommendations are absent.
identifiedIssues: CRITICAL: Hardcoded JWT secret in backend/server.js.
CRITICAL: Lack of persistent database; application uses volatile in-memory arrays.
CRITICAL: Missing input validation and sanitization on all POST/PUT API endpoints.
MAJOR: Search functionality in the Header is a UI mock and does not filter products.
MAJOR: No inventory validation during the 'Add to Cart' or 'Checkout' flows; users can exceed available stock.
MAJOR: Payment integration is mocked securely; requires actual Stripe/Payment Gateway SDK implementation.
MINOR: Missing language and currency preference toggles requested in US-001.
MINOR: Missing personalized recommendations engine; currently only displays static 'Trending' products.
MINOR: Accessibility gaps including missing ARIA labels on interactive elements and empty alt attributes on gallery images.
